actionable_feedback: Update the sensitivity checks in the workbook from +/-5% to +/-10% around each controlling threshold (implement named cells like status_plus_10pct/status_minus_10pct) and ensure those sensitivity cells feed the classification logic so borderline metrics produce a 'WARN' or 'needs_review' output when within +/-10%.

Add explicit raw-packet row identifiers in the '01_Packet_Raw' sheet (for example, columns with R1, R2 row IDs) and change all formulas to reference those row cells so the memo can cite exact row IDs (e.g., 01_Packet_Raw!R3).

Ensure all workbook checks and mode-decision logic are implemented as live spreadsheet formulas (no hard-coded intermediate overwrites) and that formula cells are the authoritative source for classifications. Avoid copying numeric results as constants into logic cells.

Run a parsing/validation step using openpyxl (or another spreadsheet library) in your CI or locally to confirm formulas exist and that cell references are parseable. Attach a short validation log or exported CSV of key sheets if automated parsing remains unavailable in the grading environment.

Update the flight_ops_memo.md to: (a) reflect the +/-10% sensitivity implementation and explain how borderline cases are handled (include the 'needs_review' criterion), (b) include explicit raw-packet row IDs for each cited packet field, and (c) explicitly list any packet fields intentionally not used and why.
After making workbook changes, re-run the artifact validation so the grader/verifier can programmatically confirm formula linkage and sensitivity behavior.

feedback_summary: Deliverables are present and provide traceable packet-field citations and a clear recommended mode (single_tracker). Primary shortcomings: (1) sensitivity checks are implemented at +/-5% but the rubric requires +/-10%, leaving borderline handling incomplete; (2) the memo uses dotted embedded_packet field identifiers but does not include explicit raw-packet row identifiers (e.g., R1, R2) for cell-level traceability; (3) the workbook could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so live formulas, explicit cell references, and linked sensitivity cells could not be independently verified. Address these to meet full rubric expectations and enable automated verification.

After that, re-run automated verification to confirm formula-level claims.
